name: Alabama Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Alabama Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

address.streetAddress: 100 N. Union St., Suite 724, Montgomery, AL 36130

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Clinical Fellowship verification
requiredDocuments.description: CF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.

requiredDocuments.name: Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Criminal background check as required by ABESPA.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Alabama SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $150, including a $100 application fee and $50 license fee. Biennial renewal is $100.

mainEntity.name: Is Alabama part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Alabama has enacted ASLP-IC legislation. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alabama through a compact privilege once the system is fully operational.

mainEntity.name: Does Alabama allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Alabama permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Alabama license or ASLP compact privilege.

mainEntity.name: Does Alabama have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Alabama participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Alabama without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Alabama?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
